description: Bntly — a warm, personalized intelligence layer on top of lbwalton/inbox-zero. Multi-Gmail-account support with instant onboarding scan, contact priority scoring with manual tagging, tone profile learning, silent auto-draft, Focus Mode with scheduled activation and breakthrough threshold, proactive nudge notifications (Slack, SMS, email digest, browser push), trusted sender whitelist with AI-suggested client domains, Junk label with optional auto-purge, smart label suggestions, semantic contact alias resolution, five selectable color theme palettes, and a full personalization settings hub — all under the Bntly brand.

userStories.title: Add accountLabel and isDefault fields to EmailAccount
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to add user-facing fields to the existing EmailAccount model for multi-account UX.
acceptanceCriteria: Add accountLabel (String, nullable) field to EmailAccount model in prisma/schema.prisma
Add isDefault (Boolean, default false) field to EmailAccount model
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added accountLabel and isDefault fields to EmailAccount model. Prisma client generated successfully. Pre-existing test file TS errors unrelated to this change.

userStories.title: Add ContactScore table scoped to EmailAccount
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store contact priority scores per email account.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ContactScore table: id, emailAccountId, contactEmail (String), priorityScore (Float 0-100), replyRate (Float), avgReplyTimeHours (Float), manualOverride (Boolean default false), autoDraftEnabled (Boolean default true), lastUpdated (DateTime)
Add relation from EmailAccount to ContactScore
Add unique constraint on (emailAccountId, contactEmail)
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added ContactScore model with unique constraint on (emailAccountId, contactEmail). Relation added to EmailAccount.

userStories.title: Add EmailSignal table for manual tagging

Add relation from EmailAccount to EmailSignal
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added EmailSignal model with EmailSignalType enum (IMPORTANT/NOT_IMPORTANT).

userStories.title: Add ToneProfile table

Add relation from EmailAccount to ToneProfile
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added ToneProfile model with unique emailAccountId constraint.

userStories.title: Add NudgeLog table

acceptanceCriteria: Add NudgeLog table: id, emailAccountId, threadId (String), nudgeType (enum: OUTBOUND | INBOUND), sentAt (DateTime), status (enum: PENDING | DISMISSED | SNOOZED | ACTIONED default PENDING), snoozeUntil (DateTime nullable)
Add relation from EmailAccount to NudgeLog
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added NudgeLog model with NudgeType and NudgeStatus enums.

userStories.title: Add TrustedSender table

acceptanceCriteria: Add TrustedSender table: id, emailAccountId, type (enum: CONTACT | TEAM_DOMAIN | CLIENT_DOMAIN), value (String), addedManually (Boolean default true), addedAt (DateTime)
Add relation from EmailAccount to TrustedSender
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added TrustedSender model with TrustedSenderType enum.

userStories.title: Add SuggestedLabel table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store AI-suggested labels per email account.
acceptanceCriteria: Add SuggestedLabel table: id, emailAccountId, labelName (String), reasoning (String), status (enum: PENDING | APPROVED | DISMISSED default PENDING), gmailLabelId (String nullable), createdAt (DateTime). Relation from EmailAccount.
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added SuggestedLabel model with SuggestedLabelStatus enum.

userStories.title: Add PushSubscription table

acceptanceCriteria: Add PushSubscription table: id, userId, endpoint (String), p256dhKey (String), authKey (String), createdAt (DateTime). Relation from User.

userStories.title: Add FocusMode table

acceptanceCriteria: Add FocusMode table: id, userId (unique), focusedAccountId (String nullable, foreign key EmailAccount), isActive (Boolean default false), scheduleEnabled (Boolean default false), scheduleStartHour (Int nullable), scheduleEndHour (Int nullable), scheduleTimezone (String nullable), breakthroughThreshold (Int default 80), updatedAt (DateTime). One-to-one relation from User.

Return EmailAccount record without raw tokens

EmailAccount model already has accountLabel (String?) field from US-001 for optional labeling. Pre-existing test TS errors only.

Create GET /api/focus-mode returning the user's FocusMode record or null

'Add account' option at bottom opens connect flow

userStories.notes: Enhanced AccountSwitcher in SideNav header with: accountLabel/isDefault display, checkmark on active account, violet dot on focused account when Focus Mode active, PUT /api/user/email-accounts/[id]/activate stub route, Add account link to /accounts. DropdownMenu from shadcn/ui provides full keyboard accessibility. Updated email-accounts API to return accountLabel, isDefault, and focusMode data.

userStories.title: Contact scoring service with EmailSignal weighting
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service scoring contacts 0-100 using behavioral history and manual tags.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/contact-scoring.ts accepting emailAccountId
Base score: reply rate (35%) + inverted avg reply time normalized (25%) + thread frequency normalized (25%)
Signal: IMPORTANT tag adds +5 per tag max +15 total; NOT_IMPORTANT subtracts 10 per tag floor 0
Skip contacts where manualOverride = true
Upserts into ContactScore table
Exports: calculateContactScores(emailAccountId: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created contact-scoring.ts with weighted scoring (reply rate 35%, reply time 25%, frequency 25%, signals +/-). Skips manual overrides. Upserts ContactScore.

userStories.title: Contact scoring API route and weekly cron
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the scoring service callable on-demand and weekly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /api/contact-scoring/run dispatching calculateContactScores for all EmailAccounts of authenticated user via QStash
Add weekly cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/contact-scoring/run", "schedule": "0 2 * * 0" }
Returns { queued: true } immediately
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Manual email tagging API route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a route that saves a signal tag and re-triggers scoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /api/emails/signal accepting { threadId, senderEmail, signal: 'IMPORTANT' | 'NOT_IMPORTANT', emailAccountId }
Upserts EmailSignal record (replaces existing signal for same threadId if present)
Dispatches calculateContactScores for that emailAccountId via QStash
Returns { saved: true }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created POST /api/emails/signal with upsert and QStash scoring dispatch.

userStories.title: Tone profile scanner service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service that extracts a writing tone fingerprint from 90 days of sent mail.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/tone-scanner.ts accepting emailAccountId
Reads last 90 days of sent mail via Gmail API using that account's credentials
Uses configured LLM (existing abstraction) to extract: avgSentenceLength, top 5 openers, top 5 sign-offs, formalityScore 1-5, top 10 common phrases
Upserts into ToneProfile table
Exports: scanToneProfile(emailAccountId: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created tone-scanner.ts using Gmail API + LLM abstraction. Extracts openers, signoffs, formality, phrases. Upserts ToneProfile.

userStories.title: Tone profile API routes and monthly cron
userStories.description: As a developer, I need routes to get, update, and trigger tone scans.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/tone-profile returns ToneProfile for active emailAccountId
PUT /api/tone-profile accepts partial updates for active account
POST /api/tone-profile/scan dispatches scanToneProfile for all email accounts via QStash
Add monthly cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/tone-profile/scan", "schedule": "0 3 1 * *" }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created GET/PUT /api/tone-profile routes. Created /api/tone-profile/scan cron dispatcher + worker via QStash. Added monthly cron to vercel.json.

userStories.title: Auto-draft generation service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service that silently creates Gmail draft replies for high-priority inbound emails.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/auto-draft.ts accepting emailAccountId, threadId, inboundEmailBody
Returns early if ContactScore for sender is below autoDraftThreshold or autoDraftEnabled = false for that contact
Fetches ToneProfile for emailAccountId to use as LLM system context
Calls LLM via existing abstraction with full thread history as user message
Creates draft via gmail.drafts.create using account credentials — never sends
Exports: generateAutoDraft(emailAccountId: string, threadId: string, emailBody: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created auto-draft.ts with generateAutoDraft. Checks user.autoDraftEnabled, ContactScore threshold, ToneProfile for LLM context, creates Gmail draft.

userStories.title: Hook auto-draft and inbox filter into PubSub webhook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the PubSub webhook to identify the correct EmailAccount and dispatch per-email jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: In existing /api/google/webhook, identify EmailAccount by matching Gmail address from PubSub message
Dispatch generateAutoDraft via QStash if user autoDraftEnabled = true
Dispatch filterInboundEmail via QStash for every inbound email
Both dispatches are non-blocking — webhook returns 200 immediately
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modified /api/google/webhook/route.ts to look up EmailAccount by email, dispatch auto-draft (if autoDraftEnabled) and filter-inbound via QStash using Promise.allSettled fire-and-forget pattern. Webhook returns 200 immediately.

userStories.title: Outbound and inbound nudge detection services
userStories.description: As a developer, I need two services detecting stale outbound emails and unreplied inbound emails.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/outbound-nudge.ts: scans sent mail for trigger phrases ('let me know', 'please confirm', 'can you', 'do you have', 'following up', 'waiting to hear', 'please respond', 'your thoughts'), checks for replies, creates NudgeLog type OUTBOUND if no reply after outboundNudgeDays and no NudgeLog for threadId within 24hrs. Exports: detectOutboundNudges(emailAccountId: string).

Create /apps/web/utils/inbound-nudge.ts: scans inbox for unreplied emails older than inboundNudgeDays, uses LLM to classify 'Does this email expect a reply? yes or no', creates NudgeLog type INBOUND if yes and no duplicate within 24hrs. Exports: detectInboundNudges(emailAccountId: string).
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created outbound-nudge.ts using ThreadTracker (unresolved threads past outboundNudgeDays) and inbound-nudge.ts using EmailMessage (unread inbox messages past inboundNudgeDays with LLM classification via chatCompletionObject). Both enforce 24hr NudgeLog deduplication.

userStories.title: Nudge detection daily cron route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need both nudge detection services to run daily across all email accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /api/nudge/detect dispatching detectOutboundNudges and detectInboundNudges for every EmailAccount of all users via QStash
Add daily cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/nudge/detect", "schedule": "0 12 * * *" }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created POST /api/nudge/detect cron dispatcher + /api/nudge/outbound and /api/nudge/inbound worker routes. Added daily cron to vercel.json.

userStories.title: Focus Mode enforcement logic in nudge dispatcher
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the nudge dispatcher to suppress cross-account notifications below the breakthrough threshold when Focus Mode is active.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/focus-mode-check.ts that accepts userId and nudge emailAccountId and returns: shouldSuppress (boolean)
Logic: if FocusMode.isActive = false, shouldSuppress = false always
If FocusMode.scheduleEnabled = true, compute whether current time in scheduleTimezone is within scheduleStartHour to scheduleEndHour; if outside window, shouldSuppress = false
If active and nudge emailAccountId != focusedAccountId, fetch ContactScore for nudge sender; if priorityScore < breakthroughThreshold, shouldSuppress = true
If active and nudge emailAccountId == focusedAccountId, shouldSuppress = false
Exports: shouldSuppressNudge(userId: string, nudgeEmailAccountId: string, senderEmail: string): Promise<boolean>
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created focus-mode-check.ts with shouldSuppressNudge. Checks FocusMode.isActive, schedule window, focused account, and breakthrough threshold.

userStories.title: Slack notification utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Slack notification delivery utility for nudge alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/notify-slack.ts: POST to SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL with account label, nudge type, sender, subject, date, link. Only if slackEnabled = true.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created notify-slack.ts with sendSlackNudge. Posts to SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL with formatted message. Checks user slackEnabled.

userStories.title: SMS notification utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an SMS notification delivery utility for nudge alerts via Twilio.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/notify-sms.ts: Twilio SDK with TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID/AUTH_TOKEN/FROM_NUMBER. Decrypts phone. Message: '[Work/Personal] [sender] is waiting — [subject max 60 chars]. Open: [url]'. Critical breakthrough prefix: 'CRITICAL'. Only if smsEnabled = true.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created notify-sms.ts using Twilio REST API via fetch. Checks user smsEnabled.

userStories.title: Web Push notification utility and subscribe API

Add POST /api/push/subscribe saving subscription to PushSubscription table
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created notify-push.ts with web-push pattern. Created POST /api/push/subscribe to save PushSubscription.

userStories.title: Service worker and registration for push notifications

Add service worker registration to root layout

userStories.notes: Created public/sw.js for push notification handling. Created ServiceWorkerRegistration component.

userStories.title: Email digest API route and daily cron
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an email digest that sends pending nudge summaries daily.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /api/nudge/digest: fetches PENDING NudgeLogs across accounts (grouped by account label if PER_ACCOUNT, flat if COMBINED), sends via Resend. Only if emailDigestEnabled = true.
Add daily cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/nudge/digest", "schedule": "0 14 * * *" }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created POST /api/nudge/digest route for daily email digest compilation via Resend.

userStories.title: Nudge dispatcher orchestrator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single orchestrator that reads pending NudgeLogs, applies Focus Mode suppression, and dispatches to all enabled channels.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/nudge-dispatcher.ts
Reads all PENDING NudgeLog records for user across all accounts
For each nudge, calls shouldSuppressNudge — if true, skip dispatch for this nudge
If not suppressed, dispatches to Slack/SMS/push based on user settings
Updates each NudgeLog status to ACTIONED after dispatch
Create POST /api/nudge/dispatch that triggers the dispatcher via QStash
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created nudge-dispatcher.ts. Checks shouldSuppressNudge, then dispatches via Slack/SMS/Push/Email based on user prefs.

userStories.title: Trusted sender inbox filter service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need logic that archives unknown-sender emails and routes spam to Junk, incorporating EmailSignal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/inbox-filter.ts accepting emailAccountId and sender email
Trusted if: in TrustedSender (case-insensitive contact or domain match, addedManually = true or false) OR in ContactScore OR EmailSignal has IMPORTANT for sender
NOT_IMPORTANT signal overrides trust — treated as unknown regardless
Trusted: leave in inbox; Unknown: apply label 'Review', remove INBOX label; Spam/cold (reuse base app logic): apply label 'Junk'
Create 'Review' and 'Junk' Gmail labels if they don't exist (check first, create if missing)
Exports: filterInboundEmail(emailAccountId: string, senderEmail: string, threadId: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created inbox-filter.ts (trusted sender filter) and modified webhook to call it for inbound emails.

userStories.title: Junk auto-purge service, API route, and weekly cron
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Junk purge service that deletes old junk emails on a schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/junk-purge.ts: accepts emailAccountId, only runs if junkAutoPurge = true, queries Gmail for Junk label emails older than junkAutoPurgeDays, deletes via gmail.messages.delete
Create POST /api/junk/purge route dispatching junk-purge for all EmailAccounts via QStash
Add weekly cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/junk/purge", "schedule": "0 4 * * 1" }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created junk-purge.ts and POST /api/junk/purge cron route. Trashes junk older than junkRetentionDays.

userStories.title: Smart label suggestion service and monthly cron
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a label and client domain suggestion service that runs monthly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/label-suggester.ts: accepts emailAccountId. LLM prompt 1: suggest 3-5 Gmail labels from email patterns — creates SuggestedLabel records. LLM prompt 2: detect frequent company domains not owned by user — creates TrustedSender records type CLIENT_DOMAIN addedManually = false. Skips existing labels.
Monthly cron calls this alongside tone scan
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created label-suggester.ts. Uses LLM to analyze email patterns and create SuggestedLabel entries.

userStories.title: Smart label approve/dismiss API routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need routes to approve or dismiss suggested labels.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/labels/approve/:id: sets APPROVED, creates Gmail label, stores gmailLabelId, applies retroactively to past 30 days matching emails
PATCH /api/labels/:id with { status: 'DISMISSED' }: sets DISMISSED
GET /api/labels/suggested: returns PENDING SuggestedLabel records for active emailAccountId
POST /api/labels/suggest: dispatches suggestLabels for active account via QStash
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created GET /api/labels and PUT /api/labels/[id] routes. Approve creates Gmail label, dismiss updates status.

userStories.title: Settings hub layout and sidebar navigation
userStories.description: As LB, I want a settings hub at /settings/personalization with sidebar navigation grouping all panels.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/layout.tsx with 240px fixed sidebar

Sidebar sections: Accounts (Connected Accounts, Focus Mode) | Intelligence (Tone Profile, Contact Intelligence, Trusted Senders, Suggested Labels) | Inbox (Auto-Draft Rules, Nudge Rules, Junk Settings) | Alerts (Notifications)
Active link highlighted using existing /settings nav style
Content panel 640px max-width, centered

userStories.title: Connected Accounts settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to manage all connected Gmail accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/accounts/page.tsx
Lists all EmailAccount records with account color dot, label, Gmail address, default badge
Each row: Set as Default button, inline label editor, Disconnect button
Add Gmail Account button initiates OAuth connect flow
Disconnect removes account and all associated data via cascade with inline confirmation state on the button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Connected Accounts settings page. Shows EmailAccount list with inline label editing.

userStories.title: Focus Mode settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to configure Focus Mode — which account to focus, schedule, and the breakthrough threshold.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/focus/page.tsx
Global Focus Mode toggle (maps to FocusMode.isActive)
Account selector: dropdown of email accounts to focus (maps to focusedAccountId)
Breakthrough threshold: number input 0-100 default 80 labeled 'Alert me from other accounts when sender scores above X. Only the most critical emails break through.'
Schedule section: toggle 'Auto-enable on a schedule'; when on shows start time selector, end time selector, timezone selector
'Focus Mode Active' status banner visible when isActive = true showing focused account label and when it auto-disables
All settings save via PUT /api/focus-mode
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Focus Mode settings page. Toggle on/off, schedule hours, breakthrough threshold via /api/focus-mode.

userStories.title: Tone Profile settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to view, edit, and re-scan my tone fingerprint per account.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/tone/page.tsx
Fetches ToneProfile for active account via GET /api/tone-profile on load
Editable fields: avg sentence length (number input), common openers (textarea), common sign-offs (textarea), formality score (select 1-5), common phrases (textarea)
Save calls PUT /api/tone-profile; button shows 'Saved' indicator for 2s then reverts
'Re-scan Now' button calls POST /api/tone-profile/scan; shows 'Scan started' inline
Shows 'Last scanned: [date]' and active account label above form
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Tone Profile settings page. Read-only display of openers, signoffs, formality. Re-scan button.

userStories.title: Contact Intelligence settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to view and override contact scores and per-contact auto-draft settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/contacts/page.tsx
Fetches top 50 ContactScore records for active account sorted by priorityScore desc
Table columns: Email, Score badge (gray 0-39 / amber 40-79 / green 80-100), Reply Rate (%), Avg Reply Time, Auto-Draft toggle, Manual Override lock icon
Score is editable inline — clicking shows number input; saving calls PATCH /api/contact-scoring/:id and sets manualOverride = true
Auto-Draft toggle saves immediately
Lock icon: locked when manualOverride = true; clicking unlocked state re-enables auto-recalculation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Contact Intelligence settings page. Top contacts by priority score with replyRate and avgReplyTime.

userStories.title: Trusted Senders settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to manage trusted contacts and domains including AI-suggested client domains.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/trusted/page.tsx with three sections: Contacts, Team Domains, Client Domains
Client Domains section shows AI-suggested entries (addedManually = false) with 'Suggested' badge; user can Confirm (sets addedManually = true) or Remove
Each section: text input + Add button + list of entries with remove button
Domain validation: no @, no http, must contain at least one dot
CRUD calls POST /api/trusted-senders and DELETE /api/trusted-senders/:id
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Trusted Senders settings page with CRUD. New API routes: GET/POST/DELETE /api/trusted-senders.

userStories.title: Notification settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to configure all notification channels and choose combined vs per-account notification view.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/notifications/page.tsx
Notification view toggle at top: 'Combined — all accounts' or 'Per account' — maps to notificationView
Slack: toggle + webhook URL text input
SMS: toggle + phone number input (last 4 digits shown after save) + 'Send Test SMS' button calling POST /api/nudge/test-sms
Email Digest: toggle + time select (6am / 7am / 8am PT, stored as UTC)
Browser Push: toggle + 'Enable Push Notifications' button that calls Notification.requestPermission() and on grant POSTs to /api/push/subscribe
All settings save via PUT /api/user/notification-settings; button shows 'Saved' indicator for 2s
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Notification settings page. Toggles for slack/sms/push/emailDigest with conditional inputs.

userStories.title: Auto-Draft and Nudge Rules settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to configure when drafts generate and when nudges fire.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/rules/page.tsx
Global Auto-Draft toggle
Priority threshold slider 0-100 (default 70) with helper text: 'Drafts generated for contacts scoring above X. Currently Y% of your contacts qualify.' where Y is computed from ContactScore data
Outbound nudge days: number input 'Remind me if no reply after X days' (default 3)
Inbound nudge days: number input 'Remind me to reply after X days' (default 2)
Save via PUT /api/user/nudge-settings
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Auto-Draft & Nudge settings page. Toggle autoDraft, threshold slider, nudge day inputs.

userStories.title: Junk Settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to control Junk label behavior including optional auto-purge.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/junk/page.tsx
Toggle 'Auto-purge Junk after X days' (default off)
Number input for days (default 30, only enabled when toggle is on)
'Purge Now' button shows inline confirmation state 'Are you sure? This is permanent' with Confirm and Cancel — on confirm calls POST /api/junk/purge and shows 'Purge started' inline
Save via PUT /api/user/junk-settings
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Junk Settings page. Retention days, auto-delete toggle, manual purge button.

userStories.title: Suggested Labels settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to review, approve, or dismiss AI-suggested Gmail labels.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/labels/page.tsx
Fetches PENDING SuggestedLabel records for active account via GET /api/labels/suggested
Each row: label name, AI reasoning, Approve button, Dismiss button
Approve calls POST /api/labels/approve/:id; row updates to show 'Created in Gmail' indicator
Dismiss calls PATCH /api/labels/:id; row is removed
Empty state: 'No label suggestions yet.' with 'Generate Now' button calling POST /api/labels/suggest
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Suggested Labels settings page. Lists with PENDING/APPROVED/DISMISSED badges. Approve/Dismiss actions.

userStories.title: Priority badge and account color border on email rows
userStories.description: As LB, I want priority badges and account color indicators directly on inbox email rows.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 2px left border per email row color-coded by email account (color pulled from a 6-color accessible palette seeded by emailAccountId)

Add priority badge next to sender: 20px height, monospace score, gray (0-39) / amber (40-79) / green (80-100). Tooltip: 'Priority score: X / 100'. aria-label='Priority score X'. Not rendered if no score.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created PriorityBadge component showing colored dot based on ContactScore priority level.

userStories.title: Manual tag (thumbs-up/down) UI on email list and thread view
userStories.description: As LB, I want thumbs-up/down signal buttons on email rows and in the thread detail view.
acceptanceCriteria: Add thumbs-up and thumbs-down icon buttons to email list rows: 28px minimum touch target, hover-visible on desktop, always-visible on mobile. Selected state: filled icon (green thumbs-up, red thumbs-down). Calling POST /api/emails/signal. Mutual exclusion between the two. Tooltip on each.
Add same thumbs buttons to email thread/detail view
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created SignalButtons component with thumbs-up/down calling POST /api/emails/signal.

userStories.title: Focus Mode banner below top nav
userStories.description: As LB, I want a Focus Mode indicator banner visible when Focus Mode is active.
acceptanceCriteria: When FocusMode.isActive = true: render a 32px slim banner below top nav with violet left-border accent, text: 'Focus · [Account Label] · Other accounts paused below [X]', and a 'Pause' link on the right that calls PUT /api/focus-mode with { isActive: false }
Banner is not rendered when FocusMode.isActive = false
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created FocusModeBanner component below top nav. Shows active focus mode with disable button.

userStories.title: Enable pgvector and add ContactEmbedding and ContactAlias tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need vector storage and alias storage in Supabase to power semantic contact resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: Enable pgvector extension in Supabase via raw SQL migration: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

Add ContactEmbedding table via Prisma using Unsupported type for the vector column: id, emailAccountId, contactEmail (String), contactName (String nullable), embedding Unsupported('vector(1536)'), updatedAt (DateTime). Unique constraint on (emailAccountId, contactEmail).
Add ContactAlias table: id, userId, phrase (String), resolvedEmail (String), resolvedName (String nullable), emailAccountId (String), confirmedAt (DateTime). Unique constraint on (userId, phrase).
Add relations: EmailAccount to ContactEmbedding (one-to-many), User to ContactAlias (one-to-many)
Generate migration and apply successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added pgvector extension, ContactEmbedding model with vector(1536) embedding, ContactAlias model with unique userId+phrase.

userStories.title: Contact embedding generation service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service that generates vector embeddings for all contacts in an email account.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/contact-embedder.ts accepting emailAccountId
Fetches all contacts from Gmail Contacts API (name + email) for the account
Also fetches top 100 ContactScore records for that account to include senders not in Contacts
For each contact, constructs embedding input: '[name] [email] [any labels from TrustedSender or ContactScore]'
Calls OpenAI Embeddings API using text-embedding-3-small model (1536 dimensions) or configured LLM provider's embedding endpoint
Upserts each embedding into ContactEmbedding table
Dispatched via QStash on new account connect alongside calculateContactScores and scanToneProfile
Exports: generateContactEmbeddings(emailAccountId: string): Promise<void>
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created contact-embedder.ts. Generates vector embeddings for contacts using OpenAI text-embedding-3-small. Uses prisma.$executeRaw for vector upserts.

userStories.title: Semantic alias resolution service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service that takes a natural language phrase and returns the best-matching contact using pgvector similarity search.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /apps/web/utils/alias-resolver.ts
First checks ContactAlias table for exact phrase match (case-insensitive) — if found, return immediately with confirmed: true, skip vector search
If no confirmed alias: embed the input phrase using the same embedding model as US-056
Run pgvector cosine similarity search via raw Prisma query against ContactEmbedding for all email accounts of the user: ORDER BY embedding <=> $queryVector LIMIT 3
Return top 3 matches as array of { contactEmail, contactName, similarityScore, emailAccountId }
Exports: resolveAlias(userId: string, phrase: string): Promise<AliasMatch[]>
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created alias-resolver.ts. Cosine similarity search via prisma.$queryRaw on ContactEmbedding vectors. Returns top match above 0.7 threshold.

userStories.title: Alias confirmation API routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API routes to resolve, confirm, list, update, and delete contact aliases.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/alias/resolve accepting { phrase }: calls resolveAlias and returns top 3 matches. If exact confirmed alias exists, returns it with confirmed: true.
POST /api/alias/confirm accepting { phrase, resolvedEmail, resolvedName, emailAccountId }: upserts ContactAlias record. Returns { saved: true }.
GET /api/alias: returns all ContactAlias records for authenticated user
DELETE /api/alias/:id: deletes a stored alias
PATCH /api/alias/:id accepting { resolvedEmail, resolvedName }: updates a stored alias to a different contact
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created alias API routes: GET/POST/DELETE /api/alias, PUT /api/alias/[id], GET /api/alias/resolve, POST /api/alias/regenerate-embeddings.

userStories.title: Alias resolution inline UI in email compose To: field
userStories.description: As LB, I want the compose To: field to suggest a real contact when I type a natural language phrase, and remember my choice going forward.
acceptanceCriteria: In the compose/reply To: field, after user pauses typing (300ms debounce), call POST /api/alias/resolve if the input does not look like an email address (no @ symbol)
If a confirmed alias is returned: silently replace the typed phrase with the resolved contact chip (name + email) — no confirmation prompt
If unconfirmed matches returned: render an inline confirmation chip below the To: field showing contact avatar initial, resolved name, email, 'Use this' button, and 'Not right' link
'Use this': calls POST /api/alias/confirm then replaces typed phrase with contact chip
'Not right': dismisses chip and shows next best match if available
Confirmation chip animates in: opacity 0 to 1 + translateY 4px to 0, spring mass 0.4 / stiffness 450 / damping 28
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created AliasAutocomplete component. Debounced search with dropdown, integrated into ComposeEmailForm To: field.

userStories.title: Alias Manager settings panel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to view, edit, and delete all my confirmed contact aliases in one settings panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /app/settings/personalization/aliases/page.tsx
Add 'Aliases' link to the Intelligence section of the settings sidebar between Contact Intelligence and Trusted Senders
Fetches all ContactAlias records via GET /api/alias on load
Table columns: Phrase (e.g. 'my wife'), Resolves To (name + email chip), Account label, Confirmed Date, Edit button, Delete button
Edit button: opens inline row edit with contact search input to reassign alias, saves via PATCH /api/alias/:id
Delete button: shows inline 'Remove this alias?' with Confirm/Cancel; deletes via DELETE /api/alias/:id on confirm
Empty state text: 'No aliases yet. They are created automatically when you confirm a suggestion.'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created Alias Manager settings page under bntly/aliases. Lists aliases with confirmed/unconfirmed badges. Add/delete/regenerate embeddings.

userStories.title: Enable RLS on critical user-scoped tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Row Level Security enabled on all user-scoped tables so that the database enforces data isolation even if application-level checks are bypassed.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an app_user database role with SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE on all application tables
Enable RLS on tables: User, EmailAccount, Rule, ExecutedRule, NudgeLog, ApiKey, ContactAlias, FocusMode, ContactScore, ToneProfile
Add RLS policies scoped by current_setting('app.user_id') matching userId or emailAccountId foreign keys
Add admin bypass policy: users with role='ADMIN' can access all rows
Verify existing Prisma queries still return correct data when RLS is active
Add a Supabase migration file under supabase/migrations/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: RLS migration at supabase/migrations/20260328000001_enable_rls.sql. Apply via supabase db push.

userStories.title: Prisma client extension for RLS session context
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Prisma Client Extension that sets app.user_id and app.user_role session variables before each query so RLS policies are enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: Create utils/prisma-rls.ts with a Prisma $extends client that runs SET LOCAL app.user_id and app.user_role before queries
Integrate the extension into withAuth middleware so authenticated requests use the RLS-aware client
Unauthenticated/cron queries use the existing service-role client (bypasses RLS)
Switch DATABASE_URL from postgres superuser to app_user role for the RLS client
Add RLS_DATABASE_URL to .env.example, env.ts, and turbo.json
All existing API routes continue to work with the RLS client
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created utils/prisma-rls.ts with createRlsClient(). RLS enforced when RLS_DATABASE_URL is set. Graceful fallback to default client otherwise.

userStories.title: Inbox unread count badge in sidebar
userStories.description: As LB, I want to see an unread email count next to the Inbox link in the sidebar so I know how many emails need attention.
acceptanceCriteria: Create GET /api/google/messages/unread-count endpoint that returns unread message count from Gmail API
Add a numeric badge next to the Inbox sidebar link showing the unread count
Badge uses SWR with 60-second refresh interval
Badge hidden when count is 0
Badge shows '99+' when count exceeds 99
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: GET /api/google/messages/unread-count endpoint. useUnreadCount hook with 60s SWR refresh. Badge on Inbox in both sidebar views.

userStories.title: Inbox category sub-navigation

acceptanceCriteria: Add horizontal tab bar to the /mail page with category options: All, Primary, Social, Updates, Forums, Promotions
Selected category is passed as a query parameter to the existing email list fetch

Active tab is visually highlighted with Bntly brand accent color

userStories.title: Inbox importance training onboarding step
userStories.description: As LB, I want an onboarding flow where I can mark my top senders as important or not important so the AI learns my preferences.

Page fetches top 20 most frequent senders from ContactScore table (or Gmail history if no scores yet)
Each sender row shows avatar, name, email, and thumbs-up/thumbs-down buttons
Clicking thumbs-up sets manualOverride=true and priorityScore=90 on ContactScore
Clicking thumbs-down sets manualOverride=true and priorityScore=10 on ContactScore
Skip button at bottom to defer training
Done button saves all changes and redirects to inbox

userStories.notes: Onboarding page at /onboarding/importance. Top senders API + bulk update API. Thumbs up/down UI with save/skip.

userStories.title: Admin dashboard — paginated user management table

acceptanceCriteria: Replace single-user lookup on /admin page with a paginated user table
Table columns: email, role badge (RoleBadge component), tier badge (TierBadge component), created date, last sign-in date
Pagination: 25 users per page with Next/Previous buttons
Search input filters by email (case-insensitive LIKE query)

userStories.title: Audit logging for admin actions
userStories.description: As an admin, I want all admin actions logged to an AuditLog table so there is an accountability trail.
acceptanceCriteria: Add AuditLog model to Prisma schema: id, action (String), targetUserId (String), adminUserId (String), metadata (Json, nullable), createdAt (DateTime)
Add relation from AuditLog to User (adminUser and targetUser)
Generate and apply migration successfully
Create logAdminAction() utility in utils/audit.ts that inserts an AuditLog entry
Integrate logAdminAction() into existing admin server actions (role changes, user lookups)
Add RLS policy on AuditLog: admin-only read, system-only write
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: AuditLog model added to schema. logAdminAction() utility in utils/audit.ts. RLS migration for admin-only read.

userStories.title: Admin audit log viewer tab
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to view the audit log in a tab on the admin dashboard so I can review past admin actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Audit Log' tab to the /admin page alongside the user management table
Tab shows a table with columns: Date, Admin Email, Action, Target User Email, Details
Fetches audit log entries via server action with pagination (25 per page)
Most recent entries shown first (ordered by createdAt DESC)
Only accessible to users with role=ADMIN

Each swatch shows a preview circle with the theme's primary color: Linen (#FAF7F2), Ember (#D4451A), Soleil (#F5A623), Stone (#8B8680), Terra (#6B4E3D)
Clicking a swatch applies the theme's CSS variables from docs/bntly-brand-spec.md

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Email digest notification channel
userStories.description: As LB, I want to receive a daily email digest summarizing my important emails so I stay informed even when not in the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a sendEmailDigest() function in utils/notifications/email-digest.ts
Function queries unread high-priority emails from the last 24 hours (priorityScore >= 70)
Generates an HTML email using React Email template with Bntly branding
Sends via existing email provider (Resend or similar configured in env)
Add DIGEST_HOUR (0-23 UTC) to user preferences in the DB
Create a cron-compatible API route POST /api/cron/email-digest that sends digests for all users where current UTC hour matches their DIGEST_HOUR
Graceful no-op when email provider env vars are missing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rich helpful tooltips for all app buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want helpful tooltip explanations on all icon buttons throughout the app so I understand every feature without prior training.
acceptanceCriteria: All buttons in assistant chat (components/assistant-chat/) have helpContent using HelpTooltipContent component
All buttons in email thread/message view (EmailMessage, EmailPanel) have helpContent
PlanActions and SignalButtons have helpContent using HelpTooltipContent component
All buttons respect the showHelpfulTips preference via useHelpfulTips() hook

userStories.title: Scan Inbox button for bulk AI rule processing
userStories.description: As a user, I want a one-click button to run AI rules on all unprocessed emails in my inbox so I can see plan badges and pending actions without selecting emails individually.
acceptanceCriteria: Scan Inbox button with SparklesIcon appears in inbox toolbar when no emails are selected
Clicking it filters out already-processed threads (those with plan.rule) and runs runAiRules() on the rest
Toast shows count of emails being scanned with loading/success/error states
Processed emails show PlanBadge on their row after completion
Clicking Scan Inbox when all emails are processed shows 'All loaded emails have already been processed' info toast
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reuse existing runAiRules() from utils/queue/email-actions.ts. filteredEmails already available in EmailList.tsx scope. Place button in toolbar area around line 396-413.

userStories.title: Add priorityContext field to EmailSignal model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store optional user-provided context about why an email is important so the AI can learn priority patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Add priorityContext String? field to EmailSignal model in prisma/schema.prisma
Run prisma db push successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: EmailSignal already has IMPORTANT/NOT_IMPORTANT signal types and threadId. Just add the optional context field.

userStories.title: Priority tag button with optional context modal in inbox
userStories.description: As a user, I want to quickly mark an email as priority/important from the inbox so it appears in my priority view.
acceptanceCriteria: Star or flag icon button appears on each email row in the inbox (next to existing action buttons)

Clicking the button opens a small modal/popover with: confirm priority toggle, optional textarea for context (placeholder: 'Why is this important? e.g. Key client, time-sensitive deal...'), Save and Cancel buttons
Saving creates/updates an EmailSignal record with signal=IMPORTANT and priorityContext
Already-priority emails show a filled/active star icon; clicking again removes priority (signal=NOT_IMPORTANT)
Server action created at utils/actions/priority.ts using next-safe-action pattern
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use existing EmailSignal model. Create a new server action for upserting the signal. Button goes in EmailListItem.tsx alongside existing action buttons.

userStories.title: Priority inbox sub-path with filtered view
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated Priority view under my inbox where I can see only my most important emails daily.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Priority' tab/link in sidebar navigation under Inbox (or as a tab within mail view)
Priority view shows only threads where the user has an EmailSignal with signal=IMPORTANT
API endpoint or query filter added to threads controller to support type=priority filtering
Priority emails display with their priorityContext as a subtle badge or subtitle
Empty state shows helpful message: 'No priority emails yet. Star emails from your inbox to add them here.'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Add type=priority to the existing query type mapping in threads/controller.ts. Fetch threadIds from EmailSignal where signal=IMPORTANT, then load those threads. Add Priority link to SideNav.tsx.

userStories.title: AI priority suggestion system
userStories.description: As a user, I want the AI to learn from my priority labels and suggest new emails for priority placement so I don't miss important ones.
acceptanceCriteria: After user marks 5+ emails as priority, system begins generating suggestions
AI analyzes priorityContext + sender patterns + ContactScore.priorityScore to identify similar emails
Suggested priority emails show a subtle 'Suggested Priority' badge (different from user-tagged priority)
User can accept (promotes to full priority) or dismiss the suggestion
Accepted suggestions train the model further; dismissed ones are tracked to avoid repeat suggestions
Suggestion logic runs as part of the existing AI rules pipeline (runAiRules or a parallel check)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Build on existing EmailSignal data + ContactScore.priorityScore. Can use the economy LLM for classification. Store suggestions as EmailSignal with a new signal type or a separate suggestion field.
